description: Post-processing verification for project context generation

verifications.name: Shorten If Too Long
verifications.description: Disabled — project context has no upper token limit; the agent targets 800-3500 tokens depending on project complexity

verifications.name: Expand If Too Short
verifications.description: Context should be at least 400 tokens

check.prompt:
Estimate token count (4 characters ≈ 1 token).

Minimum: 400 tokens

CONTENT:
{content}

Is this LESS THAN 400 tokens?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Content is TOO SHORT (<400 tokens). Expand with accurate technical details.

Strategy:
- Expand key technical points
- Add relevant architectural details
- Clarify technical decisions
- Include important constraints

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the expanded content.

verifications.name: Remove Truly Redundant Information
verifications.description: Remove genuinely duplicated content — NOT rich technical content like schemas or state machines

check.prompt:
Check if context contains genuinely DUPLICATED content — the exact same fact stated twice or more.

Do NOT flag as redundant:
- Technology Stack details
- Database schema tables and columns
- State machine transitions
- Environment variable lists
- Security, API, and architecture conventions
- Domain model entity definitions

Only flag YES if the same specific fact appears multiple times in different sections.

CONTENT:
{content}

Is there genuinely duplicated content (same fact stated more than once)?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Remove only genuinely duplicated content (same fact stated more than once).

Do NOT remove:
- Database schema, state machines, or domain model entries
- Security or API convention rules
- Environment variables
- Technology stack details

Only consolidate truly repeated statements.

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the refined content.

verifications.name: Fix Header Spacing
verifications.description: Ensure headers have proper spacing (## Header not ##Header)

check.prompt:
Check if headers are properly formatted with space after #.

Correct: ## Header, ### Title
Wrong: ##Header, ###Title

CONTENT:
{content}

Are there improperly formatted headers?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Fix ALL headers to have space after #.

##Header → ## Header
###Title → ### Title

Preserve all content.

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the corrected content.

verifications.name: Fix Unclosed Code Blocks
verifications.description: Ensure all code blocks have closing ```

check.prompt:
Check if there are unclosed code blocks (opening ``` without closing ```).

CONTENT:
{content}

Are there unclosed code blocks?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Close ALL unclosed code blocks.

Add closing ``` where missing.

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the corrected content.

check.prompt:
Check for malformed lists:
- Inconsistent indentation
- Mixed markers (- and *)
- Incorrect nesting

CONTENT:
{content}

Are there malformed lists?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Fix ALL list formatting issues.

- Use consistent markers (-)
- Fix indentation (2 spaces per level)
- Ensure proper nesting

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the corrected content.

check.prompt:
Check if sections have blank lines between them for readability.

Good:
## Section 1
Content

## Section 2

Bad:
## Section 1
Content
## Section 2

CONTENT:
{content}

Are blank lines missing between sections?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Add blank lines between ALL sections.

Ensure single blank line before each header.

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the corrected content.

verifications.name: Ensure In-Scope Defined
verifications.description: Disabled — current agent generates technical architecture sections (Technology Stack, Domain Model, Database Schema, etc.), not scope lists

verifications.name: Ensure Out-of-Scope Defined
verifications.description: Disabled — current agent generates Technical Exclusions section only when explicit exclusions are stated; scope listing is no longer part of this template

verifications.name: Remove Implementation Details
verifications.description: Remove code snippets and detailed implementation

check.prompt:
Check for LOW-LEVEL implementation details that should be removed:

VIOLATIONS (respond YES):
- Code snippets (import statements, function definitions, classes)
- Detailed algorithm pseudocode
- Step-by-step implementation instructions ("First do X, then Y")
- Specific file paths (/src/components/Header.jsx)

NOT VIOLATIONS (these are OK):
- Technology names (React, AWS DynamoDB, JWT, OAuth 2.0)
- Architecture patterns (REST API, microservices)
- High-level technical decisions
- Deployment environments
- Integration approaches

CONTENT:
{content}

Does this contain LOW-LEVEL implementation details (code, algorithms, file paths, step-by-step instructions)?

Respond ONLY 'YES' or 'NO'.

fix.prompt:
Remove ONLY low-level implementation details. Be conservative - preserve essential context.

REMOVE:
- Code snippets (functions, classes, imports)
- Detailed algorithm pseudocode
- Step-by-step implementation instructions
- Specific file paths or directory structures

PRESERVE (these are OK in context):
- Technology names (React, AWS DynamoDB, OAuth 2.0, etc.)
- Architecture patterns (microservices, REST API, etc.)
- High-level technical decisions
- Deployment environments
- Integration points
- Security approaches

If you're unsure whether to remove something, KEEP IT.

CONTENT:
{content}

Return ONLY the refined content with implementation details removed but essential technical context preserved.
